rationale: Title X is stronger overall because it gives the more specific predictor, ostial cross-sectional area, and includes the sample size plus MRI-based assessment. Those details improve scientific precision and make the title more informative for JICE readers, despite its greater length.
loser_weakness: Title Y is less specific about where cross-sectional area was measured, omits the MRI-based assessment and sample size, and has some redundancy around paroxysmal atrial fibrillation.
